content.problemStatement:
Chef John is given NNN points P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PN\mathsf{P}_1, \mathsf{P}_2, \ldots, \mathsf{P}_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč in a plane. For each valid iii, the coordinates of the point Pi\mathsf{P}_iPi‚Äč are (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi‚Äč,yi‚Äč). Help him find a vector v‚Üí=(xv,yv)\overrightarrow{v} = (x_v, y_v)v=(xv‚Äč,yv‚Äč) such that the following holds:


For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), let Si=v‚Üí‚čÖPiPi+1‚ÜíS_i = \overrightarrow{v} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathsf{P}_i \mathsf{P}_{i+1}}Si‚Äč=v‚čÖPi‚ÄčPi+1‚Äč‚Äč. Here, we define PN+1=P1\mathsf{P}_{N+1} = \mathsf{P}_1PN+1‚Äč=P1‚Äč.
The coordinates xvx_vxv‚Äč and yvy_vyv‚Äč are integers and ‚ą£xv‚ą£,‚ą£yv‚ą£‚Č§2‚čÖ109|x_v|, |y_v| \le 2 \cdot 10^9‚ą£xv‚Äč‚ą£,‚ą£yv‚Äč‚ą£‚Č§2‚čÖ109.
It is possible to find three integers www, lll and rrr (1‚Č§l‚Č§r‚Č§N1 \le l \le r \le N1‚Č§l‚Č§r‚Č§N) such that:

For each iii (l‚Č§i‚Č§rl \le i \le rl‚Č§i‚Č§r), Siw>0S_i w \gt 0Si‚Äčw>0.
For each other valid iii, Siw<0S_i w \lt 0Si‚Äčw<0.


If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one. If there are no solutions, let's define xv=yv=0x_v = y_v = 0xv‚Äč=yv‚Äč=0. (Note that the vector v‚Üí=(0,0)\overrightarrow{v} = (0, 0)v=(0,0) cannot be a valid solution.)
constraints: Subtasks**Subtask #1 (50 points, time limit 3 seconds):** N‚Č§1,000N \le 1,000N‚Č§1,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers xix_ixi‚Äč and yiy_iyi‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing two space-separated integers xvx_vxv‚Äč and yvy_vyv‚Äč.
